task: Refactored daemon loop to monitor all active projects simultaneously instead of following only the most recently updated session
discussion_summary: The daemon architecture had a critical flaw: it used `max(candidates)` to track only the single most-recently-updated stats file, causing it to abandon the first Claude session when a second one started. This session identified the root cause in `_read_stats()` and replaced the single `last_trigger_at` float with a per-project dict, enabling the daemon to poll all active projects each cycle. The fix closes the logic gap that caused leaps demo sessions to go dark when run alongside other Claude sessions in the repo.

accomplishments.what: Identified root cause: `_read_stats()` returns only max(candidates), causing daemon to track single project
Refactored daemon loop to use per-project dict instead of single `last_trigger_at` float
Modified lifecycle.py to iterate all active projects on each daemon poll cycle
Committed and pushed feat(daemon): monitor all active projects simultaneously

next_actions.why: The refactor is untested in the actual scenario that broke — need to confirm daemon no longer abandons first session when second starts

next_actions.action: Review the actual edits to lifecycle.py line 1154 area to confirm per-project dict is correctly initialized and iterated

next_actions.action: If multi-session test passes, document the daemon architecture fix in ARCHITECTURE.md or similar, noting that it now handles concurrent Claude sessions
next_actions.why: This was a significant architectural limitation that should be recorded so future work doesn't reintroduce single-project tracking

next_actions.action: Check if there are any other places in the codebase that assume single-project daemon behavior and update them

decisions.decision: Replace single `last_trigger_at` float with per-project dict keyed by project root
decisions.reason: Enables daemon to track multiple projects independently without losing state when switching between them

decisions.decision: Iterate all active projects on each daemon poll cycle instead of following max(candidates)
decisions.reason: Ensures no session goes dark when multiple Claude sessions are running concurrently

failed_approaches.approach: Single `last_trigger_at` float tracking only most-recently-updated project
failed_approaches.reason: Caused daemon to abandon first session when second Claude session started, leaving first session without daemon monitoring

relational_files.why: Contains daemon_loop and _read_stats functions that were refactored to support multi-project monitoring
Logs all operations performed this session for audit trail
